data.title: Bitcoin Shows Bullish Signals Amid Token Unlocking Events
data.description: Bitcoin is exhibiting two historical bullish signals, according to PANews. Analysts from VanEck have identified a negative funding rate and a decline in concentrated hash power as indicators of potential positive movement for the cryptocurrency. Additionally, tokens such as SUI are approaching significant unlocking events, drawing attention from several large investors. These developments are causing increased interest and activity in the market.

data.title: Base Ecosystem Token REPPO Surges to New Highs
data.description:
The Base ecosystem token REPPO has experienced a significant increase, surpassing $32 million. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the token's market value is now reported at $29.2 million, marking a 32% rise over the past 24 hours, with a trading volume of $620,000.

Previously, the token's market value began its upward trajectory from $10 million following a strategic funding commitment of $20 million from Bolts Capital to the Reppo Foundation.

BlockBeats cautions users that meme coin trading is highly volatile, often driven by market sentiment and speculative concepts, lacking intrinsic value or practical use cases. Investors are advised to be aware of the associated risks.

data.title: Study Reveals Small Percentage of Accounts Drive Prediction Market Price Discovery
data.description: Researchers from London Business School and Yale University have found that approximately 3% of accounts in prediction markets are responsible for the majority of price discovery. According to NS3.AI, the study indicates that these accounts are more adept at forecasting outcomes and reacting to news. In contrast, the majority of participants contribute to trading volume with minimal informational value.

data.title: Polymarket Market Report: Top 10 Most Volatile Markets Out of Over 20,000 Markets, Cryptocurrency Events Account for 40%
data.description: Polysights, a third-party prediction market data platform, published an article on its X platform analyzing over 20,000 Polymarket markets over the past six months and providing volatility rankings. Key findings include: - Cryptocurrency-related markets occupy four of the top ten spots, indicating high information asymmetry and few truly accurate predictions. - Deadline anxiety is prevalent; questions involving "Will X occur before [date]?" generate more volatility than "Who will win?". - Market outcomes tend to be "yes"—the probability of a "yes" outcome is 50% in markets above $1 million, compared to only 27% overall; when the market experiences significant volatility, the final outcome is more often "yes." - Mid-sized markets ($1 million to $7 million) are in a volatile range; markets above $10 million show more stable price movements due to large capital inflows.

data.title: Predicting market price discovery is primarily driven by 3% of informed traders.
data.description: A recent paper by researchers from London Business School and Yale University points out that the accuracy of market predictions is primarily driven not by "collective intelligence," but by a small number of informed traders. The study shows that only about 3% of accounts contribute significantly to price discovery and are more effective at predicting outcomes, responding to news, and improving market pricing accuracy. The paper also notes that the majority of other participants contribute the majority of trading volume, but have limited information content, and their trading losses often flow to informed traders. The research concludes that the accuracy of market predictions is more a reflection of the "wisdom of the informed minority."

data.title: Hong Kong AI Stocks Experience Decline
data.description: Hong Kong's AI stocks faced a downturn as MINIMAX-W (00100.HK) fell over 12% and Zhizhi (02513.HK) dropped more than 3%. According to Jin10, these fluctuations reflect broader market trends impacting AI-related equities.

data.title: Kalshi and Polymarket Report Similar Trading Volumes in March
data.description: Blockchain Capital partner Spencer Bogart shared on the X platform that Kalshi and Polymarket recorded nearly equal total trading volumes in March, each around $12 billion. According to Odaily, Kalshi's trading volume was $12.29 billion, while Polymarket's was $12.22 billion. Excluding sports-related transactions, Polymarket's non-sports trading volume reached $7.5 billion, compared to Kalshi's $1.6 billion. Despite their differing positions in the prediction market, both platforms are categorized similarly.

data.title: Kalshi Crypto Sector Leader: Kalshi's March trading volume exceeded $13 billion, Poly's was approximately $9.5 billion, and the Blockchain Capital partner's data is incorrect.
data.description: IcoBeast.eth, head of the Kalshi crypto section, posted on the X platform refuting previous data from Blockchain Capital partner Spencer Bogart, which stated that Kalshi's trading volume exceeded $13 billion in March, while Polymarket's trading volume was only about $9.5 billion in the same month. This data contradicts previous claims. For further discussion, please contact him directly or the Kalshi team. He also mentioned that Blockchain Capital is an investor in Polymarket. Previous report: Blockchain Capital partner: Polymarket's non-sports trading volume in March was $7.5 billion, while Kalshi's year-on-year figure was only $1.6 billion.

data.title: Crypto markets rallied across the board, with the RWA sector leading the gains at nearly 5%, and BTC breaking through $79,000.
data.description: On April 27th, according to SoSoValue data, the overall cryptocurrency market trended upward, with the RWA sector performing particularly well, rising 4.81% in the last 24 hours. Specifically, Pendle (PENDLE) rose 7.39%, Centrifuge (CFG) rose 7.17%, Ondo Finance (ONDO) and Keeta (KTA) rose 5.44% and 5.89% respectively. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) rose 2.36%, breaking through $79,000; Ethereum (ETH) rose 3.50%, approaching $2,400. Notably, MAG7.ssi rose 1.98%, DEFI.ssi rose 3.02%, and MEME.ssi rose 2.19%. Other sectors that performed well include: the NFT sector, which rose 3.85% in the last 24 hours, with Pudgy Penguins (PENGU) up 13.10%; the DeFi sector, which rose 3.74%, with Lido DAO (LDO) up 23.71%; the AI ​​sector, which rose 3.56%, with OriginTrail (TRAC) up 12.00%; and other sectors such as Meme, which rose 2.13%, with SPX6900 (SPX) up 9.79%; PayFi, which rose 1.97%, with SafePal (SFP) up 9.89%; Layer 2, which rose 1.89%, with Celestia (TIA) up 4.03%; Layer 1, which rose 1.50%, with Algorand (ALGO) up 4.16%; and CeFi, which rose 1.23%, with NEXO (NEXO) up 1.88%. The crypto sector indices, which reflect the historical performance of the sector, show that the ssiRWA, ssiNFT, and ssiDeFi indices rose by 6.37%, 4.24%, and 3.49%, respectively.

data.title: Blockchain Capital Partner: Polymarket's non-sports category transaction volume in March was $7.5 billion, while Kalshi's year-on-year data was only $1.6 billion.
data.description: Blockchain Capital partner Spencer Bogart wrote on the X platform that Kalshi and Polymarket had almost identical total trading volumes in March, both around $12 billion (Kalshi at $12.29 billion and Polymarket at $12.22 billion). Excluding sports transactions, Polymarket's non-sports trading volume was $7.5 billion, while Kalshi's was only $1.6 billion. Despite their different positioning in the prediction market, they are strangely categorized together.

data.title: Polymarket Developers: The exchange upgrade will go live at 7 PM Beijing time on April 28th, and will be down for approximately one hour.
data.description: Polymarket's official developer account, Polymarket Developers, announced on the X platform that the exchange upgrade will go live on April 28th at 11:00 AM UTC (7:00 PM Beijing time), with an estimated downtime of about one hour. The V1 order book will be cleared, all open limit orders will be cancelled, and funds and positions will be safe. Users trading via the API need to update to the latest SDK beforehand; the v1 client will cease functioning after the switch.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong New Energy Vehicle Stocks Rebound
data.description: Hong Kong's new energy vehicle stocks experienced a rebound, with BYD Company Limited (01211.HK) rising nearly 5%. According to Jin10, Chery Automobile (09973.HK) saw an increase of over 3%, while other stocks such as Great Wall Motor (02333.HK), NIO-SW (09866.HK), XPeng Inc. (09868.HK), and Leapmotor (09863.HK) also followed suit with gains.

data.title: Humanity Foundation's Token Decision May Impact Market
data.description:
The Humanity Foundation has recently asked early investors to make a binary decision regarding the allocation of $H tokens. According to ChainCatcher, blockchain monitoring data reveals that Jump Trading, a well-known market maker, transferred 50 million $H tokens to a centralized exchange on April 26, just before the deadline for investor responses.

Further blockchain records indicate that Jump Trading has been gradually transferring $H tokens since December 2025, with the total amount reaching hundreds of millions. Trix Ventures subsequently released an on-chain analysis report suggesting that Jump Trading's actions, combined with the foundation's mandatory decision, could lead market participants to price in the June 25 unlocking event ahead of time.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Telegram Introduces No-Code AI Bot Creation Tool
data.description: Telegram has launched a no-code AI bot creation tool, according to PANews. Users can interact with the LobsterFather bot to obtain a key (token) and connect it to platforms like Telewer, GPTBots, or Lazy AI. This allows users to set the bot's conversation style and functions by selecting options, supporting various AI models such as GPT and Llama. Additionally, users can create a Master Bot to manage and assign tasks to sub-bots, suitable for handling multiple chat groups or customer inquiries. Integrated with Telegram Business, the bots can autonomously respond to messages or organize communities. They can welcome new members, filter spam, and answer frequently asked questions 24/7.

data.title: USDC Emerges as Preferred AI Token for Machine Payments
data.description: USDC has become the favored token for AI-driven transactions, surpassing other tokens like FET, TAO, and RENDER. According to PANews, the x402 protocol enables AI agents to automate payments, with 98% of machine payments opting for USDC. By 2027, daily transaction volumes could reach $5 billion.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong Tech Stocks Show Strength with Baidu Leading Gains
data.description: Hong Kong's technology stocks experienced localized gains, with Baidu (09888.HK) rising over 5% and Alibaba (09988.HK) increasing by more than 1%. According to Jin10, other stocks such as Beike (02423.HK), Boss Zhipin (02076.HK), and NetEase-S (09999.HK) also followed suit with upward movements.

data.title: STOCKS | Helium Concept Stocks Surge in China Amid Supply Concerns
data.description: Helium-related stocks in China continue to rise sharply, with Jin Hong Gas increasing by over 17%, Guang Gang Gas by more than 14%, and Hua Te Gas by over 12%. Shui Fa Gas hit its daily limit, while other stocks such as Zhong Tai Shares, Jiu Feng Energy, He Yuan Gas, and Shu Dao Equipment also saw gains. According to Jin10, China Securities stated that the short- to medium-term supply from Qatar is unlikely to recover, and the uncertainty surrounding the authorization period for Russia's helium export controls is expected to exacerbate the tight supply situation in the helium market. Helium prices are anticipated to continue their upward trend.

data.title: Curve Proposes Recovery Mechanism for Bad Debt in CRV-long Market
data.description:
Curve's team has introduced a proposal on their governance forum to address approximately $700,000 in bad debt from the CRV-long LlamaLend market. According to ChainCatcher, the proposed recovery mechanism leverages the option characteristics of CRV-long treasury assets. The value of these assets increases with a rise in CRV prices, while a price drop does not further diminish their value.

The proposal suggests creating a Curve stable pool with a low amplification factor (A=2) and a high exchange fee (1%), concentrating the liquidity of treasury tokens around a 71% solvency level. Arbitrageurs can profit by purchasing treasury tokens through flash loans and partially liquidating them.

Curve DAO is invited to approve incentives for this pool, with management fees collected in the form of treasury tokens retained in the fund. If successful, this mechanism could serve as a reference solution for similar situations in the future.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank Faces Dilemma Amid Middle East Crisis, Analyst Says
data.description: On April 27, Vishnu Varathan from Mizuho Securities commented that the oil price shock caused by the Middle East conflict could disrupt demand and exacerbate existing inflation risks. According to Jin10, the Bank of Japan is expected to maintain its current policy stance this week. The central bank is in a difficult position, as it is unclear which risk will dominate and what measures should be taken in response. The high inflation and significant depreciation pressure on the yen, which existed before the Middle East crisis, limit the Bank of Japan's ability to preemptively ease policy to counter potential growth shocks. At the same time, the bank cannot raise interest rates too quickly or significantly, as this could undermine the prospects for achieving sustainable and healthy reflation.

data.title: Four Pillars Secures Series A Funding to Connect Asian and Global Markets
data.description: Four Pillars has successfully secured Series A funding from Pantera Capital and Further Ventures, achieving a valuation of 30 billion won. According to NS3.AI, the company aims to connect Asian and global markets, bridging traditional finance with Web3 technologies. Additionally, Four Pillars plans to expand its focus from research into providing solutions.
